LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSANTION INSURANCE FUND, A PUBLIC'S MOTION TO DISMISS PROCEEDING AND PETITION FOR RELIEF OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE TRANSFER, FOR LACK OF SUBJECT MATTER JURISDICTION. A R G U E D. COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT RULES AS FOLLOWS: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RENEWAL AFTER THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS FILED. ABSENT AN OPERATIVE COMPLAINT, I WOULD LARGELY BE RULING IN A VACUUM. THOUGH STATE FUND DID NOT FILE A MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER WITHIN 30 DAYS OF THE COURT'S NOVEMBER 6, 2018 ORDER, IT DID FILE ONE ON FEBRUARY 4, 2019. THE COURT'S NOVEMBER ORDER GAVE PLAINTIFFS DISCRETION TO AWAIT A RULING ON THE PROPOSED PROTECTIVE ORDER-A MARCH 29, 2019 HEARING IS SET-BEFORE FILING THEIR SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. THAT ACCOMPLISHED, THE COURT PRESUMABLY WILL HAVE THE INFORMATION NEEDED TO CONSIDER STATE FUND'S MOTION AND PETITION. PLAINTIFFS COUNSEL IS TO SUBMIT FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR# 8600 EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Proceeding And Petition For Relief Or, In The Alternative Transfer, For Lack Of Subject Matter Jurisdiction And Supporting Memorandum
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael J. Hassen, Esq., Reallaw, APC, Telephone 925-359-7500, appearing for Plaintiff Vatche Cabayan;
R. timothy O'Connor, Esq., Telephone 323-566-5321, appearing for Defendant State compensation Insurance Fund;

Ruling - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSANTION INSURANCE FUND, A PUBLIC's Motion To Dismiss Proceeding And Petition For Relief Or, In The Alternative Transfer, For Lack Of Subject Matter Jurisdiction are DENIED without prejudice to renewal after the second amended complaint is filed. Absent an operative complaint, I would largely be ruling in a vacuum.

Though State Fund did not file a motion for protective order within 30 days of the court's November 6, 2018 order, it did file one on February 4, 2019. The court's November order gave plaintiffs discretion to await a ruling on the proposed protective order-a March 29, 2019 hearing is set-before filing their second amended complaint. That accomplished, the court presumably will have the information needed to consider State Fund's motion and petition. Plaintiffs counsel is to submit form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-01-2018 Off Calendar

State Compensation Insurance Fund'S Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs' First-Amended Complaint Under California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Oct-01-2018 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael R. Hassen, Esq. of Reallaw, APC, 925.359.7500, appearing for the Plaintiffs;
R. Timothy O'Connor, Esq. of State Compensation Insurance Fund, 323.266.5321. appearing for Defendant.

DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSANTION INSURANCE FUND'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT UNDER CCP 425.16.

Matter called, ARGUMENT held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling: Defendant State Compensation Insurance Fund's anti-SLAPP motion to strike the first and second causes of action for intentional interference with prospective economic relations and breach of the implied covenant of good fair and fair dealing in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan and V. Cabayan Med. Corp. is denied as to both causes of action. Plaintiffs' allegations concerning State Fund's disparaging statements are so factually deficient that they preclude a determination whether they arise from State Fund's exercise of activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute. As explained in Central Valley Hospitalists v. Dignity Health (2018) 19 Cal.App.5th 203, the proper course is to sustain a demurrer to plaintiffs' factually deficient claims and deny this anti-SLAPP motion on the grounds that State Fund is unable to satisfy its first prong burden. Counsel for State Fund is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael R. Hassen, Esq. of Reallaw, APC, 925.359.7500, appearing for the Plaintiffs;
R. Timothy O'Connor, Esq. of State Compensation Insurance Fund, 323.266.5321. appearing for Defendant.

DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSANTION INSURANCE FUND, A PUBLIC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Matter called, ARGUMENT held. At the conclusion of argument the Court adopted the tentative ruling as modified (allowing 40 days leave to amend and allowing State Fund to bring a motion for protective order regarding the pending discovery): Defendant State Compensation Insurance Fund's demurrer to the first and second causes of action for intentional interference with prospective economic relations and breach of the implied covenant of good fair and fair dealing in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan's and V. Cabayan Med. Corp. is sustained with FORTY days leave to amend as to both causes of action. Plaintiffs' conclusory allegations fail to allege the required ultimate facts to support each of the elements of their claims. Plaintiffs are given one final opportunity to allege one or more viable claims that are not embraced by the pending appeal. STATE FUND MAY BRING A MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER REGARDING THE PENDING DISCOVERY ANY TIME WITHIN THE NEXT 30 DAYS. IF STATE FUND FILES A MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER DR. CABAYAN MAY EITHER FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT OR HE COULD WAIT UNTIL THE RESOLUTION OF THE PROTECTIVE ORDER MOTION. Counsel for State Fund is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling as modified and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Order To Seal Unredacted Portions Of Plaintiff'S Complaint And Its Attached Exhibit A Under Cal. Rules Of Court, Rule 2.551; Sanctions Under Ccp 177.5; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar May-09-2018 Continued To May-29-18 At 11:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Hassen, Esq. 415-398-8080 of Jeffer Mangels for Plaintiff Vatche Cabayan.
R. Timothy O'Connor 323-266-5321 of State Compensation Fund for Defendant STATE COMPENSANTION INSURANCE FUND.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares his views on both motions; needs to hear argument on sealing. Defendant argues, reasons all set forth in papers, met criteria, extremely prejudicial, requests a closed hearing for the 29th. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSANTION INSURANCE FUND'S Motion For Order To Seal Unredacted Portions Of Plaintiff'S Complaint And Its Attached Exhibit A Under Cal. Rules Of Court, Rule 2.551; Sanctions Under Ccp 177.5.

Defendant State Compensation Insurance Fund's motion for order to seal unredacted portions of plaintiff's complaint and for sanctions is continued to May 29, 2018 at 11:30 am to give State Fund an opportunity to substantively respond to the argument in plaintiffs' opposition memorandum that sealing of the settlement agreement is contrary to applicable law. While the court agrees with State Fund that the better course was for plaintiffs to file a motion seeking to unseal the settlement agreement rather than filing the settlement agreement as an exhibit to the complaint in a manner that makes the agreement available to the public, due to the important interest of making court files available to the public where warranted, the proper course now is for the court to address the substantive issue of whether sealing of the settlement agreement comports with California law. State Fund may file supplemental reply papers limited to that issue no later than May 21, 2018.

In addition, the document is conditionally sealed till the next hearing of 5/29/18. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Defendant to submit a proposed Interim order sealing the document till 5/29/18 in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)
